mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 7.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer spending expectations and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and contraction in housing starts indicates a supply-driven inflationary shock that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when industrial production growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month while housing starts fall below 800k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates coinciding with falling unemployment trigger equity sell-offs as investors price in higher borrowing costs and margin compression, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE move in the specified opposing directions simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly acceleration in real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, producing a persistent positive drift in equity prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between GDPC1 growth acceleration and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.15 in any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly jump in residential construction spending above 450 billion dollars signals robust credit demand and housing sector momentum, which lifts financials and materials equities and drives positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The threshold crossing event yields negative or zero 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations across a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in nonfarm payrolls and the consumer price index trigger inflation hedging flows out of equities into commodities, depressing S&P 500 prices over a one-month horizon as real yields climb.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in over 65% of co-occurring payroll and CPI increases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts paired with expanding industrial production confirms broad-based economic recovery, which lifts corporate cash flow expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day window.
failure_condition: The combined indicator fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series show month-over-month growth exceeding 1.0% for two consecutive months.
